title: Git Ignore
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries a `.gitignore` file containing patterns that tell Git which files and directories to exclude from version control. Proper ignore rules prevent build artifacts, secrets, and editor clutter from polluting the repository history.

## When to use
Use `media:gitignore;textable;list` when a pipeline node reads, generates, or merges `.gitignore` files — for example, a project scaffolder that assembles a language-specific ignore file or a security audit step checking whether sensitive paths are excluded.

## Notes
Git evaluates ignore patterns from multiple files: the repository-level `.gitignore`, per-directory `.gitignore` files, and a global user ignore file. Patterns support `*`, `**`, `?`, and negation with `!`. See also [Git Ignore](https://capdag.com/browse/media:gitignore;textable;list).
